quotes.text: Your emotional feelings are, as you know, the inner senses as they appear close to the physical plane. These are the directives. You form in your own personal perspective the data that exists within the inner self, but you do this on an individual basis in line with your own expectations, suggestions and attitudes.
quotes.translation: 如你们所知，你们的情感感受就是内在感官在接近物质层面时的显现。这些是指导方针。你们从自己的个人视角形成内在自己内部存在的数据，但你们是根据自己的期望、暗示和态度，在个人基础上这样做的。

See the 11th session, page 56. That appearance had taken place in much poorer light than tonight's event, however. This time I could see in detail the changes taking place, and their end. [Session 11 is in Vol. 1.]

quotes.text: Because Ruburt deals in words it is easy for me to communicate in this manner. That is, Ruburt automatically translates inner data given by me into coherent, valid and faithful camouflage patterns, into words. The data that I give is not actually sound on my part. The transference of my data is automatic and instantaneous on Ruburt's part and is performed through the inner workings of the mind, the inner senses and the brain.
quotes.translation: 因为鲁柏擅长处理文字，所以我很容易以这种方式沟通。也就是说，鲁柏会自动将我给出的内在数据翻译成连贯、有效且忠实的伪装模式，即文字。我给出的数据实际上并非来自我这边的声音。我的数据传递在鲁柏那边是自动且瞬间完成的，并通过心灵、内在感官和大脑的内在运作来实现。

quotes.text: Again, at best you get something like a mirror image which must be deciphered. This is rather difficult to get across to you. However, data received by the inner senses will have its own discernible impact upon the personality receiving it, and this impact is as strong as any impact caused by camouflage stimuli.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，你们最多只能得到某种必须被解读的镜像。这很难向你们解释清楚。然而，由内在感官接收的数据会对接收它的人格产生其自身可辨识的影响，而且这种影响与伪装刺激引起的任何影响一样强烈。

quotes.text: I am sorry that your tree has vanished, yet we all know that the tree has not really vanished. And I hope that you will use your inner senses to create the tree in your mind for the painting that you have begun. Now it will be a better tree in the painting, for you will be able to create the essence of the tree.
quotes.translation: 很遗憾你的树消失了，但我们都知道那棵树其实并未真正消失。我希望你能运用内在感官，在你心中为已经开始的那幅画创造出那棵树。现在画中的树会更好，因为你能创造出那棵树的本质。

There are some experiences that involve what we shall call pseudo projections.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，这正是我们内在感官的特性之一。在某些投射经验中，你也会认知或体验到一个概念，起初你可能不明白发生了什么。你通常会将想法思考成形。有些经验涉及我们所谓的伪投射。

quotes.text: Again, this is not symbolic. Only by the use of the inner senses can you obtain a viewpoint that is to some extent free of your own system. The inner self realizes the nature of its own reality, is sure of its identity and well aware of its power in the creation of actions which it projects outward from itself.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，这并非象征性的。唯有通过运用内在感官，你才能获得某种程度脱离自身系统的视角。内在自体认知到自身实相的本质，确知自己的身份，并清楚意识到自己在向外投射的行动创造中所拥有的力量。

quotes.text: It is quite true that the inner ego is aware through the inner senses of any choices that the outer ego will make with the use of free will. This does not mean that decisions are in any way predetermined. It only means that the inner ego is not bound by the dimensions in which those free will choices are made.
quotes.translation: 确实，内在自我通过内在感官能够觉察外在自我运用自由意志所做的任何选择。这并不意味着决定是预先注定的，只说明内在自我不受那些自由意志选择发生的维度所束缚。

Use of this fifth inner sense, were it available to you, and in its fuller sense -fortunately it is not--would enable you to enter into your friend.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，这涉及活组织。以你们的说法，这个类比很难理解。现在你们用外在感官试图理解亲友。若能运用这第五种内在感官——所幸你们尚未完全开发——在更完整的层面上，你们将能进入朋友的内在。

quotes.text: The knowledge then available is first of all creative, intuitive knowledge which the outer ego may translate into intellectual terms when possible. But it is no longer fearful of intuitive data. It no longer fights the inner senses, nor does it fear for its own survival in the midst of intuitional onrushes.
quotes.translation: 那时可获得的认知首先是创造性的直觉知识，外在自我在可能时会将其转化为理性术语。但它不再惧怕直觉资料，不再对抗内在感官，也不会在直觉洪流中担忧自身存续。

You must understand psychological reality, psychological time, psychological experience, and the dream existence before you can learn to utilize many abilities, since in all the mentioned aspects, you use your abilities, that is your inner senses, on a subconscious level.
quotes.translation: 我一直想探讨这一点，因为我能向你证明这个梦境世界的存在及其连续性——它始终存在于广袤的当下。你必须先理解心理实相、心理时间、心理体验以及梦境存在，才能学会运用许多能力，因为在所有提及的方面，你都在潜意识层面运用你的能力，即你的内在感官。

quotes.text: In such a state the personality is free from the limitations of a closed system concept. quotes.translation: 在这种状态下，人格摆脱了封闭系统概念的限制。虽然你可能看似忘记了自己，但身份并未真正丧失，丧失的只是身份的支撑物。正是在这种状态下，最重大且最有益的内在感官实验才会发生。

quotes.text: As you know, inner data must be perceived through the inner senses. In formulating a theory concerning the primary energy gestalt, the data is distorted by the peculiar set of outer sensory equipment characteristic of any given plane of reality. quotes.translation: 如你所知，内在资料必须通过内在感官来感知。在构建关于主要能量完形的理论时，资料会被特定实相层面特有的外在感官设备所扭曲。众所周知，人类会按照自己的形象塑造神祇。

quotes.text: Twins use communication of the inner senses all unknowing, and what Loriza read of the material moved her subconscious to intuitive realization. The communication between twins is freer than it is with others. Twins are more used to using inner communication, and instances come more easily to mind.

quotes.text: The inner senses can and do perceive this reality in an undistorted fashion. Your brain is itself a camouflage pattern. quotes.translation: 内在感官能够且确实以不受扭曲的方式感知这个实相。你们的大脑本身就是一个伪装模式，它只能翻译并感知那些看似是物质感官证据的东西，无法跳出自身之外。（微笑。）它正是它试图去探究的对象。

quotes.text: The inner senses perceive the psychic reality and transmit messages from it. We speak of these as separate, again, only for convenience, for we have but various abilities and various aspects of a self. The divisions are arbitrary. This is very important.

quotes.text: He would in no way lose consciousness of who he was, and he would perceive these experiences, again, somewhat in the same manner that you perceive heat and cold. In your camouflage pattern you must adapt yourself to the effects of heat and cold, but our man in the inner world would not be under any such obligation. I am speaking now only of our first inner sense.
quotes.translation: 他绝不会丧失对自我身份的认知，并且会以类似你们感知冷热的方式体验这些经历。在你们的伪装模式中，必须适应冷热效应的影响，但内在世界中的这个人无需承担任何此类义务。我现在仅讨论我们的第一种内在感官。

quotes.text: Without any use of this fifth inner sense no man would even come close to understanding another. This is an extremely important point, and perhaps your phrase "to put yourself in someone else's place," most clearly approximates this sense.
quotes.translation: 若不运用这第五种内在感官，没有人能真正理解另一个人。这一点极其重要，或许你们所说的"设身处地"最能近似描述这种感官。

quotes.text: You must think in terms of something being or not being, and this will get you nowhere. Everything that is, is, whether or not you can perceive it. You are equipped however to perceive far more than you do, through use of the inner senses.
quotes.translation: 你们必须摒弃"存在与否"的二元思维，否则将一无所获。一切存在之物皆真实存在，无论你是否能感知。然而通过运用内在感官，你本可感知到远比现在更多的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Direct experience in these inner senses will give you a much clearer picture of them than any words, even mine, can do. You understand however that any direct experience will be of very low power. I don't want to blast you off your feet.
quotes.translation: 对这些内在感官的直接体验，会比任何文字（包括我的话语）更清晰地展现其本质。但请明白，任何直接体验的强度都会很低——我可不想把你们震得人仰马翻。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: There is something else I wanted to mention briefly. It is that the sixth inner sense, of which we have spoken briefly, can be likened in some respects to the instincts of the inner self. This will be discussed also at our next session.
quotes.translation: 还有一点我想简要提及：我们曾简略讨论过的第六种内在感官，在某些方面可比拟为内在自我的本能。这将在下次课中继续探讨。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Through the inner senses then, you may perceive the inner laws, and remember that the inner ego knows what it is about. The intellect of the outer ego is but a pale image of your own inner ego. We will also go into this later.
quotes.translation: 通过内在感官，你得以感知内在法则，并谨记内在自我始终知晓自身使命。外在自我的智力不过是内在自我苍白无力的映像。这点我们日后也会深入探讨。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Images may begin to appear. Accept them as sights quite as valid as those you see physically. Pretend that there is an inner world, and that it will be revealed to you as you learn to perceive it with these inner senses.
quotes.translation: 影像可能会开始浮现。请接纳它们，视其与你肉眼所见之物同样真实。假装存在一个内在世界，当你学会用这些内在感官去感知时，它终将向你显明。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All true knowledge is direct experience. It cannot be classified, it cannot be named, it cannot be set down in black and white. True knowledge is only experience, direct experience, and a matter of the inner senses.
quotes.translation: 一切真知都是直接体验。它无法被归类，无法被命名，无法被白纸黑字地记录下来。真知唯有体验，是直接体验，是内在感官的事。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In the far future instruments may detect them completely. In the near future glimpses will be perceived with the use of instruments, but use and recognition of the inner senses will result in a quicker recognition.
quotes.translation: 在遥远的未来，仪器或许能完全侦测到它们。在不久的将来，借助仪器将能瞥见其端倪，但内在感官的运用与认知将带来更快的领悟。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The inner senses try desperately to make their knowledge plain to the individual. It is communicated through what you call hunch or intuition. It represents actual knowledge of a definite and inerasable reality.
quotes.translation: 内在感官竭力将其知识清晰地传达给个体。它通过你们所谓的预感或直觉来沟通。这代表着对一种明确且不可抹灭之现实的真实认知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I have also said that space travel, so-called, will of necessity deviate from its present concern with vehicles. It will be discovered that the inner senses represent your only longlasting method of such travel.
quotes.translation: 我也曾说过，所谓的太空旅行，必将脱离当前对载具的执着。届时人们会发现，内在感官才是此类旅行唯一持久的方式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: （Seth began discussing the inner ego along with the inner senses, so material on it is woven through the sessions. A few recent sessions dealing with the inner ego are: 94, 151, 162, 173. See Volumes 3 and 4.）
quotes.translation: （赛斯在讨论内在感官的同时也开始探讨内在自我，因此相关素材贯穿于各节中。近期涉及内在自我的几节包括：第94、151、162、173节。参见第三、四卷。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: You cannot displace it completely, except at your own peril. Nevertheless you can learn to trick it. You can learn to cease focusing now and then and let the inner senses look out through the ego's eyes.
quotes.translation: 你无法完全取代它，除非自取灭亡。然而你可以学会戏弄它。你可以学会不时停止聚焦，让内在感官透过自我的眼睛向外凝视。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: At some time you may be able to experience my structure. You see, the inner senses provide direct experience. The outer senses provide camouflaged distortions of translated second handed experience.
quotes.translation: 在某些时候，你或许能体验我的结构。你看，内在感官提供直接的体验。外在感官则提供经过转译的二手经验之伪装扭曲。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You will judge what you see and you will not perhaps see enough. If you perceive results using the inner senses, you will perhaps gain better knowledge of progress. Give us a moment again, please.
quotes.translation: 你会评判所见之物，或许所见不足。若你运用内在感官感知结果，或许能更清楚了解进展。请再给我们片刻时间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: I wish you both a most pleasant good evening. I would suggest that you both learn to listen and to watch in a detached fashion that will allow the inner senses to operate in a beneficial fashion.
quotes.translation: 我祝二位有个最愉快的夜晚。我建议你们学习以超然的方式倾听与观察，让内在感官以有益的方式运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: A very brief note. It is of great benefit that perceptions from the inner senses do not remain in the subconscious but become available to the intellect, and I will go into this at a future date.
quotes.translation: 简短说明：让内在感官的感知不滞留于潜意识，而是能被理智所用，这极为有益。关于这点，我将在日后详述。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Ideas and use of the inner senses can form a shaft of understanding through camouflage pattern, by which you will receive a dismally small burst of light; but even that is extremely important.
quotes.translation: 对内在感官的认知与运用，能在伪装模式中形成一道理解的通道，由此你只能接收到微弱得可怜的光束；但即便如此，这光也极为重要。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The inner senses will allow you to perceive the reality that is independent of physical form. I will ask you all to momentarily forsake your roles therefore, and to try this simple exercise.
quotes.translation: 内在感官将使你感知到独立于物质形态之外的实相。因此，我请诸位暂时放下你们的角色，尝试这个简单的练习。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To you, your body is solid. The entity can perceive the room in which you sit as if your belly were a window, but it sees with inner sight, and forms the images through the inner senses.
quotes.translation: 对你们而言，你们的身体是实体的。存有能感知你们所坐的房间，仿佛你们的腹部是一扇窗户，但它以内在视觉观看，并通过内在感官形成影像。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: During fall and the winter we will continue long sessions on the inner senses, the nature of matter, time, and also discuss other means by which you may personally experiment.
quotes.translation: 在秋季和冬季，我们将继续关于内在感官、物质本质、时间的长时间课程，并讨论你们可以亲身实验的其他方法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Almost but not quite, our invisible fuel carried to us by our inner senses could be likened to the air which you breathe, and which on a calm day is so difficult to perceive.
quotes.translation: 几乎但又不完全是，我们由内在感官运来的无形燃料，好比你们呼吸的空气——在无风的日子里，它如此难以被察觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: We will, indeed, return to our discussion of the inner senses. However there is more material concerning the nature of matter which should be considered first.
quotes.translation: 我们确实会回到关于内在感官的讨论。不过，有些关于物质本质的资料应该先加以考虑。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: I would like to discuss the inner senses in connection with some of the other material that you have been given more recently.
quotes.translation: 我想结合你们最近收到的其他资料，来讨论内在感官。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Direct experience through use of the inner senses at full capacity could be likened to the following example.
quotes.translation: 通过充分运用内在感官而获得的直接体验，可以类比为以下例子。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: （"How about my dream of the tree branch falling? Any connection there with the inner senses?"）
quotes.translation: “我梦见树枝掉落，这和内在感官有关联吗？”
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: I wanted to say more concerning the third inner sense in connection with concepts.
quotes.translation: 我想进一步谈谈与概念相关的第三内在感官。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now I would like to make a few remarks in connection with the inner senses.
quotes.translation: 现在我想就内在感官再补充几点说明。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json
